policy: Every notification source MUST register here with a documented kill switch before deployment.

sources.name: nx-dictate.service
sources.kill_switch: systemctl --user stop --now nx-dictate.service && systemctl --user disable nx-dictate.service

sources.name: nx-heartbeat.timer
sources.description: Periodic heartbeat check — was generating notification noise

sources.name: nx-guardian.timer
sources.description: Guardian monitor — was auto-starting and generating notifications

sources.name: nx-meta-monitor.timer
sources.description: Meta monitor — was auto-starting without documented kill switch

sources.name: dictation-feedback.py
sources.description: Dictation feedback — notify-send calls that couldn't be silenced
sources.kill_switch: Edit feedback.py to comment out notify-send calls, or pkill -f feedback.py

sources.name: ralph-loop-state
sources.description: Ralph loop persisted at data/ralph-state/active.md — survived restarts

sources.name: token-guard-event-stream
sources.description: Token guard toast notifications — could only be killed by restarting opencode
sources.kill_switch: Restart opencode to clear stale event streams

sources.name: plugin-toasts
sources.description: nx-plugin.js toast notifications — generated through the TUI notification system
sources.kill_switch: Restart opencode; or disable the plugin in opencode.json

rules: NO new notification source shall be added without registering here first.
Every source MUST have a documented, single-command kill switch.
Systemd services MUST NOT use Restart=always unless explicitly approved.
Every systemd timer MUST be disable-able from the dashboard.
Persistent state files MUST have a cleanup mechanism.
Plugin event streams MUST have a kill switch that does NOT require full restart.
